rulings.Case Title: SF BAY MARINAS FOR ALL, INC. VS. SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Int He Alternative, Aummary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 22, 2015, Line 4, RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In the Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Granted. No opposition filed. Respondent San Francisco Bay Conservation and Development Commission met its initial burden by showing that it is a state agency and is therefore immune from suit under 42 U.S.C. sec 1983. (Undisputed Material Facts 1-14.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SUSAN V ANDREWS et al VS. TETRA TECH EC, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Stay
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 22, 2015, Line 14, Motion To Stay. (Late tentative) The motion for stay is denied. Defendants fail to show that the federal action and state action necessarily involve the same issues. The focus of the federal action is whether defendants submitted false claims to the U.S. Gov't. The focus of this action is whether plaintiffs engaged in protected activity and were subsequently terminated because of that protected activity. It appears that plaintiffs complained about safety issues and were not necessarily complaining about fraudulent billing. Moreover, a blanket stay would be overbroad. The trial judge will be in an ideal position to determine what questions or evidence implicate the defendants' Fifth Amendment rights. It is the court's understanding that the defendants have already been deposed and did not raise any Fifth Amendment concerns at their depositions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD A DENTON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Defendant, City And County Of San Francisco [Ccsf] To Produce Esi In Native Format As Requested In Plaintiffs First Demand For Production Of Esi, And Pursuant To Ccp Â§ 2031.280
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 23, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF RICHARD DENTON's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Defendant, City And County Of San Francisco [CCSF] To Produce ESI In Native Format As Requested In Plaintiff's First Demand For Production Of ESI, And Pursuant To CCP Section 2031.280. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Argument required. Parties to appear. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JENNY DUNCAN ET AL VS. RANDALL NATHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Jenny Duncan And Jennifer Smith'S Motion Of Plaintiffs To Compel Defendant Eileen Burkes Responses To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 23, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS JENNY DUNCAN AND JENNIFER SMITH'S Motion To Compel Defendant Eileen Burke's Responses To Form Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Plaintiffs' motion is DENIED. Defendant Burke's responses to the specified Form Interrogatories were code compliant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
